# Summarize cr_cys ratio by decile
summary_by_decile <- final_master %>%
  group_by(cc_decile) %>%
  summarise(
    n      = n(),
    min    = min(cr_cys, na.rm = TRUE),
    q1     = quantile(cr_cys, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(cr_cys, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean   = mean(cr_cys, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3     = quantile(cr_cys, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    max    = max(cr_cys, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd     = sd(cr_cys, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

title: CysC / Creatinine Ratio – Project Summary
text:
Primary exposure: cr_cys_7 — binary indicator for creatinine / cystatin C ratio < 0.7
Follow-up: 90 days from baseline sample date
Framework: Competing risks (death as competing event for non-fatal outcomes)

title: CysC / Creatinine Ratio – Project Summary
text:
Inclusion

Patients in REDCap export whose EMPI appears in list.csv
Received cisplatin or carboplatin (platin_group non-missing)
Baseline cystatin C value available (cys_c_baseline non-missing)

title: CysC / Creatinine Ratio – Project Summary
text:
Exclusion

EMPI not in list: REDCap records filtered to only EMPIs present in list.csv
Missing baseline cystatin C: 3 patients excluded (cys_c_baseline is NA)
Missing platinum group: 1 patient excluded (platin_group is NA)

Final cohort: ~877 patients (from 880 in REDCap after exclusions)

title: Data Management
text:
3.1 standardize_colnames


Code
standardize_colnames <- function(df) {
  # Replace non-alphanumeric characters with underscore
  names(df) <- gsub("[^[:alnum:]_]", "_", names(df))

  # Reduce multiple underscores to a single underscore
  names(df) <- gsub("_+", "_", names(df))

  # Remove underscore if it's at the end of the name
  names(df) <- gsub("_$", "", names(df))

  # Return the cleaned dataframe
  return(df)
}

title: Data Management
Data Management
text:
3.3 create_drug_indicators
Parses regimen_dose free text to create 0/1 indicators per co-drug. Administered_alone = 1 when none of the 15 drugs are present.


Code
create_drug_indicators <- function(data) {

  # Create indicator for Bevacizumab
  data$Bevacizumab <- ifelse(grepl("Bevacizumab|Avastin", data$regimen_dose, ignore.case = TRUE), 1, 0)

  # Create indicator for Paclitaxel
  data$Paclitaxel <- ifelse(grepl("Paclitaxel|Taxol|Abraxane", data$regimen_dose, ignore.case = TRUE), 1, 0)

  # Create indicator for Pemetrexed
  data$Pemetrexed <- ifelse(grepl("Pemetrexed|pem\\b", data$regimen_dose, ignore.case = TRUE), 1, 0)

  # Create indicator for Durvalumab
  data$Durvalumab <- ifelse(grepl("Durvalumab|Durva", data$regimen_dose, ignore.case = TRUE), 1, 0)

  # Create indicator for Doxorubicin
  data$Doxorubicin <- ifelse(grepl("Doxorubicin|Adriamycin", data$regimen_dose, ignore.case = TRUE), 1, 0)

  # Create indicator for Pembrolizumab
  data$Pembrolizumab <- ifelse(grepl("Pembrolizumab|Pembro", data$regimen_dose, ignore.case = TRUE), 1, 0)

  # Create indicator for Etoposide
  data$Etoposide <- ifelse(grepl("Etoposide|etop\\b", data$regimen_dose, ignore.case = TRUE), 1, 0)

  # Create indicator for Gemcitabine
  data$Gemcitabine <- ifelse(grepl("Gemcitabine|\\bGem\\b", data$regimen_dose, ignore.case = TRUE), 1, 0)

  # Create indicator for Atezolizumab
  data$Atezolizumab <- ifelse(grepl("Atezolizumab", data$regimen_dose, ignore.case = TRUE), 1, 0)

  # Create indicator for Pertuzumab
  data$Pertuzumab <- ifelse(grepl("Pertuzumab", data$regimen_dose, ignore.case = TRUE), 1, 0)

  # Create indicator for Trastuzumab
  data$Trastuzumab <- ifelse(grepl("Trastuzumab|Traztuzumab|Transtuzumab|Herceptin",
                                   data$regimen_dose, ignore.case = TRUE), 1, 0)

  # Create indicator for Docetaxel
  data$Docetaxel <- ifelse(grepl("Docetaxel|Taxotere", data$regimen_dose, ignore.case = TRUE), 1, 0)

  # Create indicator for Fluorouracil
  data$Fluorouracil <- ifelse(grepl("Fluorouracil|5FU", data$regimen_dose, ignore.case = TRUE), 1, 0)

  # Create indicator for Methotrexate
  data$Methotrexate <- ifelse(grepl("Methotrexate", data$regimen_dose, ignore.case = TRUE), 1, 0)

  # Create indicator for Vinblastine
  data$Vinblastine <- ifelse(grepl("Vinblastine", data$regimen_dose, ignore.case = TRUE), 1, 0)

  # Create indicator for Administered alone (absence of all the above drugs)
  all_drugs <- c("Bevacizumab", "Paclitaxel", "Pemetrexed", "Durvalumab", "Doxorubicin",
                 "Pembrolizumab", "Etoposide", "Gemcitabine", "Atezolizumab", "Pertuzumab",
                 "Trastuzumab", "Docetaxel", "Fluorouracil", "Methotrexate", "Vinblastine")

  data$Administered_alone <- ifelse(rowSums(data[, all_drugs]) == 0, 1, 0)

  return(data)
}

title: Data Management
text:
3.4 cmp_variable
Builds competing-risk time and status for a single outcome. Status coding: 0 = censored, 1 = event occurred, 2 = death (competing event). If death occurs first, the death time and status (0/2) override the event.


Code
cmp_variable <- function(data, event_time, event_status, name) {
  # Enquote the time and status variables to allow their evaluation within the data context
  event_time <- enquo(event_time)
  event_status <- enquo(event_status)


  # Create dynamic names for the new columns
  cmp_time_name <- paste0("cmp_time_", name)
  cmp_status_name <- paste0("cmp_status_", name)

  # Add new columns to the dataset using mutate()
  data %>%
    mutate(
      !!cmp_time_name := ifelse(time_to_death_90 <= !!event_time ,time_to_death_90,!!event_time),
      !!cmp_status_name := ifelse(time_to_death_90 <= !!event_time,time_to_death_90_status,!!event_status)
    )
}

title: Data Management
text:
3.5 pre_lab
Finds the closest lab value on or before index_date within the time window. Returns one row per patient (most recent qualifying lab result).

See Section 6.1 for the bug found in Jiaxuan’s version of this function and how Copy 709 correctly implements it.



Code
###### pre lab  index data included closest date
pre_lab <- function(data, month, time, index_date, Group_Id) {
  index_date <- enquo(index_date)
  if (month) {
    time_value <- time * 30
  } else {
    time_value <- time
  }


  lab_name <- paste0("pre_", Group_Id, "_", time_value, "days")
  lab_date_name <- paste0("pre_", Group_Id, "_", time_value, "days_date")

  result <- data %>%
    filter(Group_Id == !!Group_Id, grepl("^\\d+\\.\\d+$", Result)) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Result)) %>%
    mutate(Result = as.numeric(Result)) %>%
    filter(Seq_Date_Time >= (!!index_date) - time_value, Seq_Date_Time <= !!index_date) %>%
    arrange(EMPI, desc(Seq_Date_Time)) %>%
    distinct(EMPI, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
    rename(!!lab_name := Result,
           !!lab_date_name := Seq_Date_Time) %>%
    select(EMPI,
           !!lab_name, !!lab_date_name)

  return(result)
}

title: Data Management
text:
3.6 post_lab_less_than_cutpoint
Finds the first post-baseline lab value below a clinical cutpoint within days. Used for post-baseline lab cutpoint outcomes.


Code
## post lab cutoff function
post_lab_less_than_cutpoint <- function(data,days,index_date,Group_ID,Group_ID_name,outcome_name,cutpoint) {
  index_date <- enquo(index_date)
  lab_name <- paste0("post_", Group_ID_name, "_cutpoint_", days, "days")
  lab_date_name <- paste0("post_", Group_ID_name, "_cutpoint_", days, "days_date")

  result <- data %>%
    filter(Group_Id %in% Group_ID) %>%
    filter(grepl("^[><]?\\d+\\.?\\d* ?$",Result)) %>%
    mutate(Result=as.numeric(parse_number(Result))) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Result)) %>%
    filter(Seq_Date_Time > (!!index_date), Seq_Date_Time <= !!index_date + days) %>%
    filter(Result<cutpoint) %>%
    arrange(EMPI,Seq_Date_Time) %>%
    distinct(EMPI, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
    rename(!!lab_name := Result,
           !!lab_date_name := Seq_Date_Time) %>%
    select(EMPI,
           !!lab_name, !!lab_date_name)
  return(result)
}

Code
Cockcroft <- function(age, weight, male, cre) {
  if (male == 0) {
    value <- (140 - age) * weight * 0.85 / (72 * cre)
  } else {
    value <- (140 - age) * weight / (72 * cre)
  }
  return(value)
}




Code
# CKD-EPI 2012 creatinine-based eGFR (race-free CKD-EPI equation)
ckd_epi_gfr <- function(cre, male, age) {
  k <- ifelse(male == 1, 0.9, 0.7)
  a <- ifelse(male == 1, -0.302, -0.241)
  m <- ifelse(male == 1, 1, 1.012)
  gfr <- 142 * min(c(cre/k, 1))^a * max(c(cre/k, 1))^-1.2 * 0.9938^age * m
  return(gfr)
}




Code
# CKD-EPI 2012 cystatin C-based eGFR (Inker et al. 2012)
# Age coefficient = 0.996 per official NKF/Inker 2012 (not 0.9961, which is the 2021 Cr+CysC value)
ckd_epi_cys_gfr <- function(cys, male, age) {
  gfr <- 133 * min(c(cys/0.8, 1))^(-0.499) * max(c(cys/0.8, 1))^(-1.328) *
         0.996^age * ifelse(male == 0, 0.932, 1)
  return(gfr)
}




Code
# Combined creatinine + cystatin C eGFR — exact coefficients from R code summary.Rmd
# Note: coefficients differ from CKD-EPI 2021 race-free (a=-0.144/-0.219, max_cr=-0.544,
# min_cys=-0.323, max_cys=-0.778, age=0.9961, female=0.963)
ckd_epi_cre_cys_gfr <- function(creatinine, cystatin_c, male, age) {
  k      <- ifelse(male == 1, 0.9, 0.7)
  a      <- ifelse(male == 1, -0.144, -0.219)
  female <- ifelse(male == 0, 0.963, 1)
  gfr_cr_cys <- 135 *
    min(c(creatinine/k, 1.0))^a  *
    max(c(creatinine/k, 1.0))^(-0.544) *
    min(c(cystatin_c/0.8, 1.0))^(-0.323) *
    max(c(cystatin_c/0.8, 1.0))^(-0.778) *
    0.9961^age * female
  return(gfr_cr_cys)
}




Code
# Adjust indexed eGFR for body surface area
unindex <- function(value, bsa) {
  return(value * bsa / 1.73)
}

# All eGFR variables computed via mapply() (scalar functions over rows).
# Uses PDF names baseline_creatinine and cys_c_baseline (renamed in section 5.8).

    baseline_cre_egfr            = mapply(ckd_epi_gfr, baseline_creatinine, male, age),
    baseline_cys_c_egfr          = mapply(ckd_epi_cys_gfr, cys_c_baseline, male, age),
    ckd_epi_gfr_cre_cys          = mapply(ckd_epi_cre_cys_gfr,
                                          baseline_creatinine, cys_c_baseline, male, age),
    cockcroft                    = mapply(Cockcroft, age, weight, male, baseline_creatinine),
    baseline_cre_egfr_unindexed  = mapply(unindex, baseline_cre_egfr, BSA),
    baseline_cys_c_egfr_unindexed = mapply(unindex, baseline_cys_c_egfr, BSA),
    ckd_epi_gfr_cre_cys_unindex  = mapply(unindex, ckd_epi_gfr_cre_cys, BSA)
  )




Code
# Post-baseline AE lab outcomes (from RPDR lab — original pull).
# Dates are already Date type from section 4.7; no further conversion needed.

Anemia_g2_value <- post_lab_less_than_cutpoint(
  data = master_lab, days = 90, index_date = baseline_sample_date,
  Group_ID = "HGB", Group_ID_name = "Anemia_2", cutpoint = 10)   # HGB < 10 g/dL

Anemia_g3_value <- post_lab_less_than_cutpoint(
  data = master_lab, days = 90, index_date = baseline_sample_date,
  Group_ID = "HGB", Group_ID_name = "Anemia_3", cutpoint = 8)    # HGB < 8 g/dL

Throm_g2_value <- post_lab_less_than_cutpoint(
  data = master_lab, days = 90, index_date = baseline_sample_date,
  Group_ID = "PLT", Group_ID_name = "Throm_2", cutpoint = 75)    # PLT < 75×10^9/L

Throm_g3_value <- post_lab_less_than_cutpoint(
  data = master_lab, days = 90, index_date = baseline_sample_date,
  Group_ID = "PLT", Group_ID_name = "Throm_3", cutpoint = 50)    # PLT < 50×10^9/L

# AKI grade 2: post-baseline CRE > 1.5 × pre-baseline CRE (KDIGO)
AKI_g2_value <- master_lab %>%
  merge(pre_CRE_45days, by = "EMPI", all.x = TRUE) %>%
  filter(Group_Id == "CRE") %>%
  filter(grepl("^[><]?\\d+\\.?\\d* ?$", Result)) %>%
  mutate(Result = as.numeric(parse_number(Result))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Result), !is.na(pre_CRE_45days)) %>%
  filter(Seq_Date_Time > baseline_sample_date,
         Seq_Date_Time <= baseline_sample_date + 90) %>%
  filter(Result > 1.5 * pre_CRE_45days) %>%
  arrange(EMPI, Seq_Date_Time) %>%
  distinct(EMPI, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  select(EMPI, Result, Seq_Date_Time) %>%
  rename(post_AKI_2_cutpoint_90days      = Result,
         post_AKI_2_cutpoint_90days_date = Seq_Date_Time)

# AKI grade 3: post-baseline CRE > 3 × pre-baseline CRE (KDIGO)
AKI_g3_value <- master_lab %>%
  merge(pre_CRE_45days, by = "EMPI", all.x = TRUE) %>%
  filter(Group_Id == "CRE") %>%
  filter(grepl("^[><]?\\d+\\.?\\d* ?$", Result)) %>%
  mutate(Result = as.numeric(parse_number(Result))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Result), !is.na(pre_CRE_45days)) %>%
  filter(Seq_Date_Time > baseline_sample_date,
         Seq_Date_Time <= baseline_sample_date + 90) %>%
  filter(Result > 3 * pre_CRE_45days) %>%
  arrange(EMPI, Seq_Date_Time) %>%
  distinct(EMPI, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  select(EMPI, Result, Seq_Date_Time) %>%
  rename(post_AKI_3_cutpoint_90days      = Result,
         post_AKI_3_cutpoint_90days_date = Seq_Date_Time)

# Merge AE outcomes into final_master
final_master <- final_master %>%
  merge(Anemia_g2_value, by = "EMPI", all.x = TRUE) %>%
  merge(Anemia_g3_value, by = "EMPI", all.x = TRUE) %>%
  merge(Throm_g2_value,  by = "EMPI", all.x = TRUE) %>%
  merge(Throm_g3_value,  by = "EMPI", all.x = TRUE) %>%
  merge(AKI_g2_value,    by = "EMPI", all.x = TRUE) %>%
  merge(AKI_g3_value,    by = "EMPI", all.x = TRUE)

5.1 Mortality variables


Code
# days_last_follow_up: numeric days from baseline to last known follow-up date
final_master <- final_master %>%
  mutate(days_last_follow_up = as.numeric(last_follow_up_date - baseline_sample_date))

# decode pt_status (PDF name kept) to character label
final_master <- final_master %>%
  mutate(pt_status = case_when(
    pt_status == 1 ~ "Alive",
    pt_status == 2 ~ "On Hospice",
    pt_status == 3 ~ "Dead"
  ))

# time_to_death_90: follow-up time capped at 90 days
# time_to_death_90_status: 0=alive/censored, 2=dead (competing-risk coding)
# death_90: binary 0/1
final_master <- final_master %>%
  mutate(
    time_to_death_90 = ifelse(days_last_follow_up > 90, 90, days_last_follow_up),
    time_to_death_90_status = case_when(
      days_last_follow_up > 90                          ~ 0,
      days_last_follow_up <= 90 & pt_status == "Dead"  ~ 2,
      days_last_follow_up <= 90 & pt_status != "Dead"  ~ 0
    ),
    death_90    = ifelse(time_to_death_90_status == 2, 1, 0)
  )

Code
# cr_cys: raw ratio (creatinine / cystatin C)
final_master$cr_cys <- final_master$baseline_creatinine / final_master$cys_c_baseline

# cr_cys_cat: four ordered categories
final_master <- final_master %>%
  mutate(cr_cys_cat = case_when(
    cr_cys < 0.5                  ~ 1,
    cr_cys >= 0.5 & cr_cys < 0.7 ~ 2,

    cr_cys >= 0.9                 ~ 4

final_master$cr_cys_cat <- factor(
  final_master$cr_cys_cat,
  levels = c("1", "2", "3", "4"),
  labels = c("< 0.5", "0.5-0.7", "0.7-0.9", "≥ 0.9")
)

# cr_cys_7: primary binary exposure — 1 if cr_cys < 0.7
final_master$cr_cys_7 <- ifelse(final_master$cr_cys < 0.7, 1, 0)

# Alternative eGFR-based exposures
final_master <- final_master %>%
  mutate(
    # 30% discrepancy: cystatin eGFR vs creatinine eGFR (CKD-EPI)
    exposure_epi_30 = ifelse(baseline_cys_c_egfr < 0.7 * baseline_cre_egfr, 1, 0),

    # 15% discrepancy: combined unindexed eGFR vs Cockcroft
    exposure_cg_15  = ifelse(ckd_epi_gfr_cre_cys_unindex < 0.85 * cockcroft, 1, 0),
    # Sensitivity ratio: combined unindexed eGFR / Cockcroft
    egfr_combined_cg_ratio = ckd_epi_gfr_cre_cys_unindex / cockcroft
  )

section: 5.6 BMI, age categories, ICI, CKD stage
text:
5.6 BMI, age categories, ICI, CKD stage


Code
# bmi: calculated from height (cm) and weight (kg)
# bmi_cat: 1=underweight <18.5, 2=normal 18.5–25, 3=overweight 25–30, 4=obese ≥30
# age_cat: 1=≤65, 2=66–80, 3=>80
# ici: immunotherapy co-administration (Atezolizumab OR Pembrolizumab OR Durvalumab)
# ckd_stage_cg: CKD stage derived from Cockcroft-Gault eGFR
final_master <- final_master %>%
  mutate(
    bmi = weight / (height / 100)^2,
    bmi_cat = case_when(
      bmi < 18.5              ~ 1,
      bmi >= 18.5 & bmi < 25 ~ 2,
      bmi >= 25   & bmi < 30 ~ 3,
      bmi >= 30               ~ 4,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    age_cat = case_when(
      age <= 65             ~ 1,
      age > 65 & age <= 80 ~ 2,
      age > 80              ~ 3
    ),
    ici = ifelse(Atezolizumab == 1 | Pembrolizumab == 1 | Durvalumab == 1, 1, 0),
    ckd_stage_cg = factor(case_when(
      cockcroft >= 90              ~ 1,
      cockcroft >= 60 & cockcroft < 90 ~ 2,
      cockcroft >= 30 & cockcroft < 60 ~ 3,
      cockcroft >= 15 & cockcroft < 30 ~ 4,
      cockcroft < 15                   ~ 5
    ))
  )

5.7 Label REDCap coded variables


Code
# Apply human-readable labels to REDCap numeric-coded variables (per data dictionary)
final_master <- final_master %>%
  mutate(
    sex        = factor(sex, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Male", "Female")),
    race       = factor(race, levels = 1:5, labels = c("White", "Black", "Asian", "Other", "Unknown")),
    ethnicity  = factor(ethnicity, levels = 1:3, labels = c("Non-Hispanic", "Hispanic", "Unknown")),
    tumor_stage = factor(tumor_stage, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Local/Early", "Advanced/Metastatic")),
    platin_group = factor(platin_group, levels = 1:3, labels = c("Carboplatin", "Cisplatin", "Unknown")),
    # ecog_score: REDCap codes 1=ECOG 0, 2=ECOG 1, ..., 7=N/A
    ecog_score = case_when(
      ecog_score >= 1 & ecog_score <= 6 ~ ecog_score - 1,  # remap to actual ECOG 0–5
      ecog_score == 7 ~ NA_real_,                           # N/A → missing
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    ecog_estimate = case_when(
      ecog_estimate >= 1 & ecog_estimate <= 6 ~ ecog_estimate - 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )

text:
5.8 Cancer type categories (7 groups)


Code
# cancer_type_cat_7: recode REDCap tumor_type (numeric 1–9) into 7 clinical groups
# REDCap coding (from data dictionary):
#   1 = Lung → Thoracic
#   2 = Breast
#   3 = Upper GI/esophagus/gastric  → GI
#   4 = Lower GI/colon/rectum      → GI
#   5 = Genitourinary/RCC/bladder   → GU
#   6 = Mesothelioma                → Other
#   7 = Gyn/ovarian/uterus/cervix  → GYN
#   8 = HEENT/Laryngeal/head & neck → HEENT
#   9 = Other                       → Other
final_master <- final_master %>%
  mutate(
    cancer_type_cat_7 = factor(case_when(
      tumor_type == 1             ~ 1,   # Thoracic (Lung)
      tumor_type == 2             ~ 2,   # Breast
      tumor_type %in% c(3, 4)    ~ 3,   # GI (Upper + Lower)
      tumor_type == 5             ~ 4,   # GU
      tumor_type == 7             ~ 5,   # GYN
      tumor_type == 8             ~ 6,   # HEENT
      tumor_type %in% c(6, 9)    ~ 7,   # Other (Mesothelioma + Other)
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ), levels = 1:7, labels = c("Thoracic", "Breast", "GI", "GU", "GYN", "HEENT", "Other"))
  )

title: Data Management
text:
5.8 Platinum dosing variables


Code
# carbo / cis: binary platinum type (platin_group is now a labeled factor)
# carbo_dose: carboplatin AUC (from platin_dose); cis_dose: cisplatin mg/m²
# 3 carboplatin records have platin_dose and dosage_mg swapped (total mg in
# platin_dose, AUC in dosage_mg): records 758, 786, 836. Fix by swapping back.
# cis_group: cisplatin dose tier (1=<40, 2=40–60, 3=≥60 mg/m²)
# dose_1: high-dose flag (carbo AUC≥5 OR cisplatin≥75 mg/m²)
# carbo_AUC_cat: 1=AUC≤4, 2=AUC≥5, 0=not carboplatin
# cis_dose_cat: 1=<75 mg/m², 2=≥75, 0=not cisplatin
final_master <- final_master %>%
  mutate(
    carbo        = ifelse(platin_group == "Carboplatin", 1, 0),
    cis          = ifelse(platin_group == "Cisplatin",   1, 0),
    # Fix 3 swapped records: platin_dose > 10 for carboplatin means mg was
    # entered in the AUC field; their dosage_mg contains the actual AUC
    carbo_dose   = case_when(
      carbo == 1 & platin_dose > 10 ~ as.numeric(dosage_mg),  # swap back
      carbo == 1                     ~ platin_dose,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    cis_dose     = case_when(cis == 1 ~ platin_dose, TRUE ~ NA_real_),
    cis_group    = case_when(
      cis_dose < 40                    ~ 1,
      cis_dose >= 40 & cis_dose < 60  ~ 2,
      cis_dose >= 60                   ~ 3,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    dose_1 = factor(case_when(
      carbo_dose >= 5 | cis_dose >= 75 ~ 1,
      carbo_dose <  5 | cis_dose <  75 ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )),
    carbo_AUC_cat = case_when(
      carbo_dose <= 4 ~ 1,
      carbo_dose >= 5 ~ 2,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),
    cis_dose_cat = case_when(
      cis_dose <  75 ~ 1,
      cis_dose >= 75 ~ 2,
      TRUE ~ 0
    )
  )

5.12 HIV from RPDR (hiv_rpdr)


Code
# hiv_rpdr: HIV status from RPDR diagnoses + medications (Jiaxuan's pipeline).
# Note: There is no hiv column in the REDCap CSV. The hiv variable in Copy 709 / cleaned_master
# was always derived from RPDR. Jiaxuan's final code Rmd used the name hiv_new.
#
# Step 1: dia_list_name with HIV terms, excluding false-positive diagnosis names
# Step 2: Further exclude patients whose HIV diagnosis co-occurs with hepatitis B/C

no_hiv <- c(
  "1 - Hives-Oncall",
  "Contact with and (suspected) exposure to human immunodeficiency virus [hiv]",
  "Encounter for screening for human immunodeficiency virus [hiv]",
  "counseling",
  "Inconclusive laboratory evidence of human immunodeficiency virus [hiv]",
  "Asymptomatic human immunodeficiency virus [hiv] infection status",
  "Family history of human immunodeficiency virus [hiv] disease",
  "HIV exposure-Oncall"
)

hiv_terms <- c("hiv", "human immunodeficiency virus")

hiv_med_list <- c(
  "abacavir", "ziagen", "emtricitabine", "emtriva", "lamivudine", "tenofovir", "viread",
  "zidovudine", "retrovir", "doravirine", "pifeltro", "efavirenz", "sustiva", "etravirine",
  "intelence", "nevirapine", "viramune", "rilpivirine", "edurant", "atazanavir", "reyataz",
  "darunavir", "prezista", "fosamprenavir", "lexiva", "ritonavir", "norvir", "saquinavir",
  "invirase", "tipranavir", "aptivus", "enfuvirtide", "fuzeon", "maraviroc", "selzentry",
  "cabotegravir", "vocabria", "dolutegravir", "tivicay", "raltegravir", "isentress", "fostemsavir",
  "rukobia", "ibalizumab", "trogarzo", "lenacapavir", "sunlenca",
  "atripla", "biktarvy", "cabenuva", "cimduo", "combivir", "complera", "descovy", "dovato",
  "epzicom", "evotaz", "genvoya", "juluca", "kaletra", "odefsey", "prezcobix", "stribild",
  "symtuza", "triumeq", "truvada", "trizivir",
  "didanosine", "stavudine", "videx", "zerit", "delavirdine", "rescriptor", "indinavir",
  "crixivan", "nelfinavir", "viracept", "fortovase"
)

hiv_rpdr_initial <- dia_list_name(
  master_dia,
  listed_names  = hiv_terms,
  excluded_names = no_hiv,
  med_data       = master_med,
  med_names      = hiv_med_list,
  index_date     = baseline_sample_date
)

# Step 2: Further filter — exclude patients whose HIV diagnosis also mentions hepatitis B/C
hiv_rpdr_cleaned <- hiv_rpdr_initial %>%
  left_join(master_dia %>% select(EMPI, Diagnosis_Name), by = "EMPI") %>%
  filter(
    str_detect(Diagnosis_Name, regex("hiv|human immunodeficiency virus", ignore_case = TRUE)),
    !str_detect(Diagnosis_Name, regex("hepatitis B|hepatitis C", ignore_case = TRUE))
  ) %>%
  distinct(EMPI)

final_master <- final_master %>%
  mutate(hiv_rpdr = ifelse(EMPI %in% hiv_rpdr_cleaned$EMPI, 1, 0))

title: Data Management
text:
5.14 Charlson Comorbidity Index


Code
### Charlson comorbidity index
ici_diag <- dia %>% subset(select = c(EMPI, Diagnosis_Name, Date, Code, Code_Type))
ici_diag <- ici_diag %>% merge(final_master %>% select(EMPI, baseline_sample_date),
                                by = "EMPI", all.y = TRUE)
ici_diag$Date <- as.Date(ici_diag$Date, "%m/%d/%Y")
ici_diag_pre  <- subset(ici_diag, Date < baseline_sample_date)

# Leukemia and lymphoma must be extracted manually (ICD code map includes them under cancer)
leukemia <- ici_diag_pre[grep("leukemia", ici_diag_pre$Diagnosis_Name, ignore.case = TRUE), ]
leukemia <- leukemia[!grepl("lymphoma", leukemia$Diagnosis_Name, ignore.case = TRUE), ]
leukemia <- as.data.frame(table(leukemia$EMPI))
names(leukemia)[names(leukemia) == "Var1"] <- "EMPI"
names(leukemia)[names(leukemia) == "Freq"] <- "leukemia"
leukemia$leukemia[leukemia$leukemia > 0] <- 1

lymphoma <- ici_diag_pre[grep("lymphoma", ici_diag_pre$Diagnosis_Name, ignore.case = TRUE), ]
lymphoma <- lymphoma[!grepl("leukemia", lymphoma$Diagnosis_Name, ignore.case = TRUE), ]
lymphoma <- as.data.frame(table(lymphoma$EMPI))
names(lymphoma)[names(lymphoma) == "Var1"] <- "EMPI"
names(lymphoma)[names(lymphoma) == "Freq"] <- "lymphoma"
lymphoma$lymphoma[lymphoma$lymphoma > 0] <- 1

leuk_lymph <- merge(leukemia, lymphoma, by = "EMPI", all = TRUE)

# Compute comorbidity scores separately for ICD-9 and ICD-10
diag_icd9  <- subset(ici_diag_pre, Code_Type == "ICD9",  select = c("EMPI", "Code"))
diag_icd10 <- subset(ici_diag_pre, Code_Type == "ICD10", select = c("EMPI", "Code"))
diag_icd9  <- diag_icd9[!(diag_icd9$Code == ""), ]
diag_icd10 <- diag_icd10[!(diag_icd10$Code == ""), ]

charlson9  <- comorbidity(x = diag_icd9,  id = "EMPI", code = "Code",
                           map = "charlson_icd9_quan",  assign0 = FALSE)
charlson10 <- comorbidity(x = diag_icd10, id = "EMPI", code = "Code",
                           map = "charlson_icd10_quan", assign0 = FALSE)

charlson <- merge(charlson9, charlson10, by = "EMPI", all = TRUE)
charlson[is.na(charlson)] <- 0

# Take maximum across ICD-9 / ICD-10 for each domain
charlson$ami      <- pmax(charlson$mi.x,       charlson$mi.y)
charlson$chf      <- pmax(charlson$chf.x,      charlson$chf.y)
charlson$pvd      <- pmax(charlson$pvd.x,      charlson$pvd.y)
charlson$cevd     <- pmax(charlson$cevd.x,     charlson$cevd.y)
charlson$dementia <- pmax(charlson$dementia.x,  charlson$dementia.y)
charlson$copd     <- pmax(charlson$cpd.x,      charlson$cpd.y)
charlson$rheumd   <- pmax(charlson$rheumd.x,   charlson$rheumd.y)
charlson$pud      <- pmax(charlson$pud.x,      charlson$pud.y)
charlson$mld      <- pmax(charlson$mld.x,      charlson$mld.y)
charlson$diab     <- pmax(charlson$diab.x,     charlson$diab.y)
charlson$diabwc   <- pmax(charlson$diabwc.x,   charlson$diabwc.y)
charlson$hp       <- pmax(charlson$hp.x,       charlson$hp.y)
charlson$rend     <- pmax(charlson$rend.x,     charlson$rend.y)
charlson$canc     <- pmax(charlson$canc.x,     charlson$canc.y)
charlson$msld     <- pmax(charlson$msld.x,     charlson$msld.y)
charlson$metacanc <- pmax(charlson$metacanc.x, charlson$metacanc.y)
charlson$aids     <- pmax(charlson$aids.x,     charlson$aids.y)

charlson <- charlson %>%
  subset(select = c(EMPI, ami, chf, pvd, cevd, dementia, copd, rheumd, pud, mld,
                    diab, diabwc, hp, rend, canc, msld, metacanc, aids))
charlson$canc <- 1  # all patients have cancer by definition

charlson <- merge(charlson, leuk_lymph, by = "EMPI", all = TRUE)
charlson[is.na(charlson)] <- 0

ages <- final_master %>% subset(select = c(EMPI, age))
charlson <- merge(charlson, ages, by.x = 1, by.y = 1)

# Weighted Charlson score with age adjustment (Quan 2011)
charlson$score <-
  charlson$ami + charlson$chf + charlson$pvd + charlson$cevd +
  charlson$dementia + charlson$copd + charlson$rheumd + charlson$pud +
  ifelse(charlson$mld == 1 & charlson$msld == 0, 1, 0) +
  ifelse(charlson$msld == 1, 3, 0) +
  charlson$diab + charlson$diabwc +
  charlson$hp * 2 + charlson$rend * 2 + charlson$canc * 2 +
  charlson$metacanc * 4 + charlson$aids * 6 +
  charlson$leukemia * 2 + charlson$lymphoma * 2 +
  ifelse(charlson$age >= 50 & charlson$age < 60, 1, 0) +
  ifelse(charlson$age >= 60 & charlson$age < 70, 2, 0) +
  ifelse(charlson$age >= 70 & charlson$age < 80, 3, 0) +
  ifelse(charlson$age >= 80, 4, 0)

# cci: categorical severity
charlson_sc <- charlson %>% subset(select = c(EMPI, score))
charlson_sc$cci <- ifelse(charlson_sc$score < 3, "mild",
                   ifelse(charlson_sc$score >= 3 & charlson_sc$score < 5, "moderate", "severe"))

final_master <- final_master %>% merge(charlson_sc, by = "EMPI", all.x = TRUE)

text:
5.15 Competing risk variables


Code
# Apply cmp_variable() to build cmp_time_* and cmp_status_* for each outcome.
# AE outcomes (anemia, throm, aki, grade_2/3_aes) are now computed from RPDR pipeline
# (section 4.9) and their competing-risk variables are computed here.

# Adverse event competing risk
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master, event_time = time_to_anemia_2, event_status = Anemia_g2_90days, name = "anemia_2")
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master, event_time = time_to_anemia_3, event_status = Anemia_g3_90days, name = "anemia_3")
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master, event_time = time_to_throm_2,  event_status = Throm_g2_90days,  name = "throm_2")
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master, event_time = time_to_throm_3,  event_status = Throm_g3_90days,  name = "throm_3")
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master, event_time = time_to_aki_2,    event_status = AKI_g2_90days,    name = "aki_2")
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master, event_time = time_to_aki_3,    event_status = AKI_g3_90days,    name = "aki_3")
# Composite AE: use the earliest event time across anemia/throm/AKI
final_master <- final_master %>%
  mutate(
    time_to_grade_2_aes = pmin(time_to_anemia_2, time_to_throm_2, time_to_aki_2, na.rm = TRUE),
    time_to_grade_3_aes = pmin(time_to_anemia_3, time_to_throm_3, time_to_aki_3, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master, event_time = time_to_grade_2_aes, event_status = grade_2_aes, name = "grade_2_aes")
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master, event_time = time_to_grade_3_aes, event_status = grade_3_aes, name = "grade_3_aes")

# Platinum-related hospitalization (side effects: hosp reasons 1–7)
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master,
  event_time = time_to_side_effect, event_status = side_effect, name = "side_effect")

# Hospitalization by reason 1–11
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master, event_time = time_to_event_hosp_1,  event_status = event_hosp_1,  name = "hosp_1")
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master, event_time = time_to_event_hosp_2,  event_status = event_hosp_2,  name = "hosp_2")
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master, event_time = time_to_event_hosp_3,  event_status = event_hosp_3,  name = "hosp_3")
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master, event_time = time_to_event_hosp_4,  event_status = event_hosp_4,  name = "hosp_4")
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master, event_time = time_to_event_hosp_5,  event_status = event_hosp_5,  name = "hosp_5")
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master, event_time = time_to_event_hosp_6,  event_status = event_hosp_6,  name = "hosp_6")
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master, event_time = time_to_event_hosp_7,  event_status = event_hosp_7,  name = "hosp_7")
# hosp_8–11 use their own event_status
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master, event_time = time_to_event_hosp_8,  event_status = event_hosp_8,  name = "hosp_8")
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master, event_time = time_to_event_hosp_9,  event_status = event_hosp_9,  name = "hosp_9")
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master, event_time = time_to_event_hosp_10, event_status = event_hosp_10, name = "hosp_10")
final_master <- cmp_variable(final_master, event_time = time_to_event_hosp_11, event_status = event_hosp_11, name = "hosp_11")


# Convert all cmp_status_* columns to factor (required by tidycmprsk)
final_master <- final_master %>%
  mutate(across(contains("cmp_status_"), as.factor))

# Save final_master for use by analysis.qmd
saveRDS(final_master, "final_master.rds")
